data.current_description:
Revoke an Authentiq ID using email & phone.

If called with `email` and `phone` only, a verification code 
will be sent by email. Do a second call adding `code` to 
complete the revocation.
data.summary_suggestion: Revoke Authentiq ID via email and phone verification
data.description_suggestion: Initiates revocation of an Authentiq ID using email and phone verification. First call with email and phone parameters triggers a verification code sent to the provided email address. Second call must include the verification code to complete the revocation. Returns 200 on successful revocation.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description lacks clarity about the two-step process, expected parameters, and response details. Added explicit summary, clarified the two-step verification flow, specified what parameters are required at each step, and documented the response behavior for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description:
Register a new ID `JWT(sub, devtoken)`

v5: `JWT(sub, pk, devtoken, ...)`

See: https://github.com/skion/authentiq/wiki/JWT-Examples
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new Authentiq ID by registering a self-signed JWT containing subject (sub) and device token (devtoken). The JWT format varies by API version: v4 uses JWT(sub, devtoken) while v5 uses JWT(sub, pk, devtoken, ...). Returns 201 with the registered Authentiq ID. Refer to JWT-Examples documentation for payload structure details.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description is cryptic with version-specific details mixed in without clear explanation. Added clear summary, explained what the operation does (creates new ID), clarified the JWT format requirements, documented the 201 response code, and contextualized version differences for better AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Revoke an Identity (Key) with a revocation secret
data.description_suggestion: Revokes an Authentiq ID (Identity/Key) using a revocation secret. Requires the public key (PK) as path parameter and the revocation secret as request parameter. Returns 200 on successful revocation. This method provides direct revocation without requiring email verification.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description lacks input parameter details and output information. data.current_description:
update properties of an Authentiq ID.
(not operational in v4; use PUT for now)

v5: POST issuer-signed email & phone scopes in
a self-signed JWT

See: https://github.com/skion/authentiq/wiki/JWT-Examples
data.description_suggestion: Updates properties of an Authentiq ID by posting issuer-signed email and phone scopes in a self-signed JWT. Requires the public key (PK) as path parameter and an AuthentiqID object in the request body. Returns 200 with the updated Authentiq ID. Note: Not operational in v4; use PUT method instead. See JWT-Examples for payload structure.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description is confusing with version-specific caveats and unclear JWT requirements. Added clear summary, specified the PK path parameter, documented the request body schema (AuthentiqID), clarified the JWT format and scopes, documented the 200 response, and reorganized version notes for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description:
Update Authentiq ID by replacing the object.

v4: `JWT(sub,email,phone)` to bind email/phone hash; 

v5: POST issuer-signed email & phone scopes
and PUT to update registration `JWT(sub, pk, devtoken, ...)`

See: https://github.com/skion/authentiq/wiki/JWT-Examples
data.summary_suggestion: Replace Authentiq ID with updated object
data.description_suggestion: Replaces an Authentiq ID object entirely using a PUT request. Requires the public key (PK) as path parameter and an AuthentiqID object in the request body containing updated subject, public key, device token, and other registration details. Returns 200 with the updated Authentiq ID. JWT format varies by version: v4 uses JWT(sub,email,phone) for email/phone binding; v5 uses JWT(sub, pk, devtoken, ...). See JWT-Examples for payload structure.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description is confusing with version-specific details mixed together. Added clear summary indicating full replacement semantics, specified the PK path parameter, documented the AuthentiqID request body schema, clarified the JWT format differences by version, documented the 200 response, and reorganized version notes for better AI agent interpretability.

data.description_suggestion: Initiates a push-based authentication sign-in request by submitting a PushToken JWT. The server sends an authentication challenge to the user's registered device. Expects a self-signed PushToken in JWT format containing authentication credentials. Returns a 200 response with the authentication request status. Used to trigger out-of-band authentication flows.

message: Operation 'sign_request' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, not_actionable, poor_ai_interpretability. data.description_suggestion: Submits a scope verification request by posting a Claims JWT to initiate verification of user claims within a specific scope. Expects a self-signed or issuer-signed Claims JWT containing the claims to be verified. Returns a 201 Created response with the verification job details and job identifier. Initiates an asynchronous verification process that can be monitored via the returned job ID.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks clarity about what 'scope verification' means and what the operation accomplishes. Missing details about Claims input format, the 201 response indicating resource creation, and that this starts an asynchronous process. Enhanced with domain context about JWT claims, response status code meaning, and job tracking capability.

message: Operation 'sign_delete' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is minimal and lacks context about side effects. Missing explanation of what happens after deletion, the job parameter requirement, and typical use cases. Added details about job termination, state changes, and cleanup context for AI agents to understand when to use this operation.

data.description_suggestion: Cancels and deletes a verification job identified by the job parameter. Removes the job from the system and terminates any ongoing verification process. Returns a 200 OK response. After deletion, the job can no longer be queried or updated. This operation is typically used to clean up abandoned or expired verification requests.
data.reasoning: Original description is minimal and lacks context about side effects. Missing explanation of what happens after deletion, the job parameter requirement, and typical use cases. Added details about job termination, state changes, and cleanup context for AI agents to understand when to use this operation.

data.current_description: get the status / current content of a verification job
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve verification job status and content
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current status and content of a verification job identified by the job parameter. Returns a 200 OK response containing the job's verification state, claims data, and any completion results. Use this operation to poll the progress of an ongoing verification process or retrieve final verification results after job completion.
data.reasoning: Original description is somewhat clear but lacks context about the job parameter and response content. Missing explanation of what data is returned and typical polling patterns. Enhanced with details about job identification, response structure, and use cases for monitoring verification progress.

data.current_description: HEAD to get the status of a verification job
data.description_suggestion: Performs a HEAD request to check the status of a verification job identified by the job parameter without retrieving the full response body. Returns HTTP headers containing job status information and metadata. Use this lightweight operation to efficiently poll job status without downloading the complete verification data. Useful for monitoring job progress with minimal bandwidth.
data.reasoning: Original description doesn't explain why HEAD method is used or its benefits. Missing context about what headers are returned and when to use HEAD vs GET. This operation finalizes the verification workflow and triggers any post-verification actions.

data.current_description:
authority updates a JWT with its signature
See: https://github.com/skion/authentiq/wiki/JWT-Examples
data.summary_suggestion: Sign verification job with authority signature
data.description_suggestion: Updates a verification job by adding an authority signature to the JWT. The authority (issuer) signs the verification claims with its private key, converting the JWT from self-signed to issuer-signed format. Expects the job parameter identifying the verification job and a signed JWT in the request body. Returns a 200 OK response with the updated, authority-signed JWT. This operation is performed by the issuing authority to validate and sign verified claims.
data.reasoning: Original description assumes domain knowledge about JWT signing and authority roles. Missing explanation of what 'authority updates' means, the input/output format, and the role of issuer signatures. Enhanced with clarity about JWT signing process, the distinction between self-signed and issuer-signed tokens, and the authority's role in the verification workflow.
